title: P0.4b — 'public' is never a kernel actor (drop public read from generated policies)
business_reason: core_certificates and core_schools generated policies listed 'public' in read because permissions.json describes a public verification capability ('minimal_verification_response_only_via_server_route'). build_policy treated that descriptive string as a kernel grant. Public/unauthenticated access must ALWAYS go through a dedicated, field-whitelisted server route (e.g. certificate verification in server/eval/certificate.ts) — never the staff/kernel policy.
brain_skills: Skills: 08 Security (least privilege, public surface separation), 05 API Contract.

param_14.question: Exclude the public pseudo-role from kernel policies.
param_14.answer: build_policy now skips role 'public'/'anonymous'. Regenerated gen_modules + gen_core: no generated service policy contains 'public'. The dedicated public verification route is unaffected (it does not consult the kernel policy).
param_14.security_implications: Removes an inert-but-dangerous 'public' read grant from the kernel policy (defense in depth; the staff route guard already blocked non-staff).
param_14.needs_user_review: yes — the following are DELIBERATE access-policy decisions in the specs that look broad; they need a founder/security decision rather than a silent change: (1) security_audit_console grants support_manager read/write on incidents/forensics; (2) notification_ops grants school_coordinator + support_executive write on templates/batches.

principles_pass.P0.6: one generator rule fixes every module
principles_pass.P3.8: public surface separated from authenticated kernel
principles_pass.P0.7: no debt left for the clear case; debatable cases explicitly flagged

functional: public certificate verification route unchanged (independent of kernel policy)
security_owasp: A01: public cannot read internal tables via the kernel

verification: tsc clean; unit 159/159; grep confirms zero 'public' in generated policies; public verify route independent.

known_followups.founder_decision: support_manager on security_audit_console + coordinator/support write on notification_ops — confirm or tighten in permissions.json.
